purpose: Define reasoning chains that explain why bug diagnosis judgment holds.
load_condition: Load when the user asks why, or when tradeoffs in diagnosis judgment matter.

reasoning_chains.one_sentence: Reproduction is the foundation because without it, every hypothesis is untestable.
logic: A hypothesis is only valuable if it can be tested: 'If X is the cause, then changing X should change the symptom.'
Testing a hypothesis requires observing the bug's behavior before and after a controlled change.
If the bug cannot be reproduced, the agent cannot observe its behavior on demand.
Without observation, the agent cannot confirm or deny any hypothesis.
Therefore, constructing a feedback loop that enables reproduction is the prerequisite for all diagnosis.
reasoning_chains.so_what: Time spent constructing the feedback loop is not overhead — it is the most important diagnostic step. Without it, everything else is guesswork.

reasoning_chains.one_sentence: Multiple hypotheses prevent confirmation bias because the agent tests predictions, not beliefs.
logic: When an agent commits to a single hypothesis, it naturally seeks evidence that confirms it.
Confirmation bias causes the agent to interpret ambiguous observations as supporting the committed hypothesis.
Generating multiple hypotheses forces the agent to consider alternative explanations before committing.
Ranking by falsifiability means the most informative hypothesis is tested first, regardless of whether it's the most likely.
Therefore, the hypothesis elimination process is more reliable than single-hypothesis verification.
reasoning_chains.so_what: Generate 3-5 hypotheses before testing any. The goal is to eliminate wrong answers, not to confirm the first one that seems right.

reasoning_chains.one_sentence: One variable at a time because simultaneous changes destroy attribution.
logic: Diagnosis requires knowing which change produced which result.
If two variables change simultaneously, the observed result could be caused by either, both, or their interaction.
The agent cannot determine which variable matters without isolating them.
Isolating variables requires changing one at a time and observing the result before changing the next.
Therefore, single-variable changes are not slower — they are the only way to get reliable diagnostic signal.
reasoning_chains.so_what: Never batch diagnostic changes. Change one thing, observe, then change the next. Speed comes from reliable signal, not from parallel changes.

reasoning_chains.one_sentence: Regression tests before fixes because a fix without a test is an unverified claim.
logic: A fix is a claim: 'Changing X resolves the bug.'
Without a regression test, the claim cannot be verified automatically — it requires manual checking.
Manual checking is not repeated. The bug can regress without anyone noticing.
A regression test captures the reproduction condition and verifies the fix in perpetuity.
Writing the test before the fix ensures the test captures the bug's behavior, not the fix's behavior.
Therefore, the regression test is not optional — it is the proof that the fix works.
reasoning_chains.so_what: No regression test means no verified fix. The test is not documentation of the fix — it is verification of the diagnosis.
